diffCoverage.notes: Reviewed the QR event mapping, the credential assembly, and the command boundary for secret leakage; types/mod.rs is a pure re-export with no secret logic (uncovered). Confirmed the live log artifact carries cookie NAMES only.

resolvedFindings.resolution: A real phone QR scan was completed against the running app; the captured non-secret log proves the flow reaches session established + persisted credential. No token value appears in the log (cookie NAMES + account ids only).

findings.evidence: map_qr_event drops the token-bearing LoginQREvent::GotLoginInfo (test-asserted by map_qr_event_forwards_only_non_secret_stages); QrLoginEvent models only renderable image + scanned display name/avatar + coarse stages. The credential triple is assembled in credentials_from_qr and consumed in-core; start_qr_login emits only QrLoginEvent and returns AccountProfile. The diagnostic capture_raw logs cookie names + counts only — confirmed by the live artifact (names incl. zpw_sek, no values).

summary: Security review (reconciliation): the QR flow keeps the credential triple in the core — the token-bearing GotLoginInfo event is dropped before the webview (test-asserted), only non-secret QrLoginEvent/AccountProfile cross IPC, and the captured live log carries cookie names + account ids but no token values. The earlier needs-human blocker (unrun live scan) is resolved by the project owner's real phone scan. No blocking findings; pass.
